text.title: Engineering Ladder and Growth Plan Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Engineering Ladder and Growth Plan application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Default to 5 levels (L1 entry, L2 emerging, L3 senior, L4 staff, L5 principal). ≤3 levels = no progression visibility; ≥7 levels = over-engineered.
rule.rationale: Industry consensus (Etsy, Square, Lattice published ladders): 5 levels is the sweet spot for teams 5-200.

rule.statement: Each level has 3-5 concrete artifact examples (PRs, designs, postmortems). Not adjectives ('takes ownership'); examples.
rule.rationale: Adjective ladders are unreviewable; example ladders create shared definition.

rule.statement: Each engineer has a written growth plan reviewed quarterly. Plan has 2-3 specific goals + artifact targets.
rule.rationale: Annual growth plans are stale by quarter 2; quarterly is the cadence that lets the plan steer behavior.

rule.statement: Promo packet contains: artifacts at next level (per r2), manager assessment, ≥2 peer endorsements. Decision committee reviews packet, not vibes.
rule.rationale: Vibes-based promos correlate with bias + politics; evidence packet creates fairness + reviewability.

rule.statement: Ladder is published internally. New hires + existing engineers can see it. Private ladders fuel speculation + attrition.
rule.rationale: Transparency literature: visible career paths reduce voluntary attrition 20-40%.
